title.sub: The page was not found. The motive is unclear.

cookies.agreement: By using this site, you agree to the
cookies.noFutureAppear: This message won't appear again in the future.
cookies.use: This website uses a few cookies to remember your preferred language and whether you saw this message.

language.description: Change the language used on the website. If no translation is available, English will be used instead.
language.title: Language

theme.description: Choose a color palette where blue and yellow are the main accent colors to better account for colorblindness.
theme.title: Theme

layoutChanges.boldDescription: If you only believe what you see with your own eyes, this update is for you.
layoutChanges.description: Content on desktop is now properly centered, left and right margins on mobile have been decreased and hovering over text links makes you want to do so for the rest of your life. I also rewrote some parts of the website and implemented components.
layoutChanges.title: A shiny new domain.

newDomain.boldDescription: Have you ever seen a single word of German around here? Ganz genau.
newDomain.description: It always bothered me a bit that the top-level domain of this website was .de, even though this website is less about me being German and more about the things I create. Don't worry, pprmint.de will redirect you to the new domain for a while.
newDomain.title: A shiny new domain.

title.sub: I make things look neat with vector points, keyframes and colorful monospace letters.

contact.text: If you have any other privacy-related questions just send an email to privacy[at]pprmint.art.
contact.title: Any questions?

text.long: On your first visit, the preferred language of your browser is detected and written to the "localStorage". If you change the language from the Settings menu, this value will be overwritten. These and future preferences are read each time you visit this site, so you don't have to set them up again and again.
text.tldr: Settings such as your preferred language are, as the name suggests, stored locally in your browser's "localStorage" so you don't have to set them up again and again. Per definition, I guess this also counts as a "cookie".

externalLinks.text: Some pages may contain links to external websites. Any link that takes you to another website will have a blue arrow and, when you hover over it, a blue background. Once you visit such external websites, you are subject to their respective privacy policies. I take no responsibility for the practices nor content of those other websites.
externalLinks.title: External links.

responsible.text: Responsible for content on this website is me, Nicklas Scharp. For privacy-related questions, feel free to send an email to privacy[at]pprmint.art.
responsible.title: Person responsible.

accessTime.description: The time when a client got a file from the server. If you care: The format's DD/MMMM/YYYY: HH:MM:SS +GMT.
accessTime.title: Access time.

agent.description: This thing tells the server infos like what browser, operating system and rendering engine is used. I can also tell you that Windows 11 is still referred to as "(Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)" in there. For more info on what's in the User-Agent header, see:
agent.title: User-Agent.

domain.description: Imagine that: It's all pprmint.art. Who knew.
domain.title: Domain.

file.description: The path and name of the file that the client received, like the favicon, fonts and other assets.
file.title: Received file.

httpCodes.description: Usually that's "200 OK", because everything's awesome. If you wanna learn more about all the different codes that the HTTP world has to offer, see:
httpCodes.title: HTTP code.

ip.description: Nope, I got no clue where you live. Not that I give a damn anyway. The law requires my hosting provider to anonymize IP addresses, so the last octet of every IP address is replaced by a random value. I can confirm: The IP address that appears in the logs doesn't match my actual address, so that's cool.
ip.title: IP address.

storagePeriod.description: Each log entry is stored for up to 7 days.
storagePeriod.title: Storage period.

usage.description: Earlier I mentioned that statistics are created with this data. That's it. For a statistics fanboy like yours truly, this is of course a dream come true and most interesting to see that 0.3% (tried to) access the site on Windows XP. That's about as useful as it gets for me. The statistics are interesting to look at (to me at least), but I'm not selling the logs on eBay or anything.
usage.title: Usage for me.

text.tldr: Like most other web servers, this one collects some data about your browser when you visit this site. My hosting provider creates some nice statistics with this data, but that's about it for my usefulness of this data.

update.accentColors: Updated accent colors and those of the hunger bar.
update.textureBeacon: Updated texture for beacon UI to have another divider line.
update.textureButtonIcons: Updated textures for some icon buttons to move on hover.
update.textureLogos: Updated Mintcraft logos on loading and title screen.

vanillatweaks.description: JSON files to fix some text colors. Remember how I said this pack was only compatible with 3 languages? Yeah that was fixed with this.
vanillatweaks.name: Vanilla Tweaks

do.headline: Do:
do.modifyOnlineContent: Use this pack, its add-ons or your modification in online content, like YouTube videos.
do.modifyPersonalUse: Use and modify Mintcraft and/or its add-ons to your liking and for personal use.
do.optionalCredit: Credit me somewhere when you use my pack by linking to this website.
do.serverPacks: Use this pack or parts of it for server resource packs. Remember to properly credit Vanilla Tweaks if you do.
do.sharePrivate: Share this pack, or your modified version of it, with friends privately.

dont.headline: Don't:
dont.redistribute: Host, mirror, sell or otherwise redistribute versions, parts and/or modifications of Mintcraft and/or its add-ons anywhere public for others to download.

addonFont.description: A font that I specifically made to use with Mintcraft. You can see it used in the screenshots further up on this page, as well as in these pack icons.
addonFont.name: Mintbit font add-on

addonSounds.description: Despite this add-on being named after the plural of a word, this pack actually just replaces one sound, which is the button click sound. This one is similar, but less jarring.
addonSounds.name: Sounds add-on

mintcraft.description: My resource pack providing you with dark-mode containers and a selfish-color palette and many other tweaks. Some icons even move when you hover over them!
mintcraft.name: Mintcraft

steps.downloadPacks: Scroll down and click on a download button.
steps.moveToTop: Move my packs to the top of the list. If you want. I dunno what you like to use them with.
steps.placeInFolder: Place my packs in your resource pack folder. You can find yours by going to Options, Resource Packs and clicking on Open Pack Folder.

splashes.sub: All the splashes seen in Mintcraft.

downloads.description: If you could actually read the text on slide 3 in a sensible order, you deserve a download button.
downloads.headline: Mintsans V2

mintalt.cardText: I looked at Google's Material icons, noticed some had text in them, and went "Huh." The good: Numbers. The bad: That stupid name. And the page for this font. That's still in the works.

mintcraft.cardText: What started as a simple "I don't like the colors of the default Minceraft UI" ended with me trying to make the UI look more modern, changing up the colors and a bit of the iconography.

mintsans.cardText: My first attempt at creating a geometric font family. A more optimized version is in development and will take at least thrice as long as it has taken me to make the following page.

startmenutiles.cardText: Branding is a pain. That's why everyone has their own that works best for them. With these 32 start menu tiles I made ages ago, I wanted to make them all feel at least somewhat more unified. And also fix Adobe's bullshit icons.

title.sub: Look at some of my works or download stuff.
There's nothing to see here yet.
